title: Built, Not Born
author: Tom Golisano
subline: Every great company is built — never born.
customHTML:
<svg viewBox="0 0 1600 2400" preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid slice" style="position:absolute;inset:0;width:100%;height:100%">
  <!-- A scaffold / steel-frame skyscraper blueprint rising from the top, constructed from hairlines. The single red element is a hard-hat silhouette in the upper right of the build. -->
  <g stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="1" fill="none">
    <!-- Outer frame -->
    <rect x="500" y="320" width="600" height="1100"/>
    <rect x="540" y="360" width="520" height="1020"/>
    <!-- Vertical studs -->
    <line x1="620" y1="320" x2="620" y2="1420"/>
    <line x1="700" y1="320" x2="700" y2="1420"/>
    <line x1="780" y1="320" x2="780" y2="1420"/>
    <line x1="860" y1="320" x2="860" y2="1420"/>
    <line x1="940" y1="320" x2="940" y2="1420"/>
    <!-- Horizontal beams (every 120px) -->
    <line x1="500" y1="440" x2="1100" y2="440"/>
    <line x1="500" y1="560" x2="1100" y2="560"/>
    <line x1="500" y1="680" x2="1100" y2="680"/>
    <line x1="500" y1="800" x2="1100" y2="800"/>
    <line x1="500" y1="920" x2="1100" y2="920"/>
    <line x1="500" y1="1040" x2="1100" y2="1040"/>
    <line x1="500" y1="1160" x2="1100" y2="1160"/>
    <line x1="500" y1="1280" x2="1100" y2="1280"/>
  </g>
  <!-- Foundation: a thick black base -->
  <rect x="460" y="1420" width="680" height="40" fill="#1a1a1a"/>
  <!-- Crane silhouette: a tower crane rising from upper-right, the only structural element that extends outside the frame -->
  <g stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="2" fill="none">
    <line x1="1200" y1="1420" x2="1200" y2="200"/>
    <line x1="1200" y1="200" x2="600" y2="200"/>
    <line x1="1200" y1="200" x2="1500" y2="320"/>
    <line x1="780" y1="200" x2="780" y2="280"/>
    <!-- Cable -->
    <line x1="780" y1="280" x2="780" y2="600"/>
  </g>
  <!-- The hook: a single red L-bracket + block lifting a beam into place (the only red — 'you build it') -->
  <g stroke="#fc1c46" stroke-width="3" fill="#fc1c46">
    <rect x="755" y="600" width="50" height="20"/>
    <line x1="780" y1="620" x2="780" y2="680" stroke-width="2"/>
    <rect x="700" y="680" width="160" height="30"/>
  </g>
  <!-- Coordinate label, blueprint style -->
  <text x="500" y="1480" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="14" font-weight="500" fill="#4c4c4c" letter-spacing="0.18em">PLAN · NORTH ELEVATION</text>
  <text x="500" y="1510" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="14" font-weight="400" fill="#4c4c4c" letter-spacing="0.18em">SCALE 1:200</text>
  <!-- Title block label, bottom of the drawing -->
  <text x="1100" y="1510" text-anchor="end" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="14" font-weight="500" fill="#4c4c4c" letter-spacing="0.18em">PROJECT NO. 014</text>
</svg>
rationale: A blueprint-elevation drawing of a building scaffold dominates the upper 2/3. A tower crane reaches in from the right and is lifting a single red I-beam into place — the only chromatic element, the act of 'building' made literal. Below the drawing sits a tight 360px mixed-case 'Built, / Not Born' title. Blueprint labels (PLAN, SCALE, PROJECT NO.) reinforce the construction metaphor.
